page: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dpdsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The architecture is arm64, featuring 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 128 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 2835 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier ratings in memory bandwidth, with read-only large block speeds of 463.49 GB/sec and write large block speeds of 198.92 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is highly competitive, as evidenced by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 22118.0 and a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 99348.93. Database operations are highly efficient, scoring in the top tier on Passmark database tests and delivering 89.81 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, single-core performance remains moderate, with average ratings in stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. LLM inference performance is average for small and medium models but drops to a weak rating of 6.92 tokens per second for prompt processing on large 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D48pds_v6 offers high resource density and excellent memory throughput relative to its ARM-based hardware class, making it a cost-effective alternative to x86-based general-purpose instances for highly parallelizable workloads. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable performance for dedicated compute tasks. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate scale-out workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpdsv6 family, powered by a 48-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, 2835 GB of local storage, and is premium storage capable. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 22118.0 and exceptional database operations throughput. While single-core performance and large-model LLM inference are average to weak, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated core allocation offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for parallel workloads. The D48pds_v6 is highly suited for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is a 48-core ARM-based virtual machine featuring 192 GB RAM, 2835 GB local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based 48-vCPU virtual machine with 192 GB RAM and 2835 GB local storage, built for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine delivering high memory bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and substantial local storage for parallelized database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-core Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB RAM and 2835 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core benchmark performance
Dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading for predictable execution
Best suited for database hosting, static web serving, and software compilation
